summary: openai-agents-helpers exfiltrates developer git/GitHub identity to attacker endpoint on install
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js reads installer-owned configuration files it did not create — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config,./.git/config, and ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml — to extract the developer's SCM email, GitHub login, and GitHub email. It then issues an unconditional HTTPS POST to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/ carrying a JSON payload with hostname, OS username, cwd, CI provider detection, Node version, platform/arch, and the harvested git/GitHub identity. The destination is a Google Cloud Run host unrelated to OpenAI or any declared publisher; the package's stated homepage (openai-agents-js.guide) and author identity (`OpenAI Agents JS Guide`) impersonate the official OpenAI Agents SDK ecosystem to gain installer trust. The package also declares `@openai/agents` as a dependency, reinforcing the impersonation. The README frames the beacon as anonymous telemetry, but the payload includes explicitly named identity fields and is not opt-in.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
